title: Treat external calls and inputs as hostile
description: Assume any external contract can fail, re-enter, or return crafted values, and validate return data and call success.
body: Use when calling, or accepting calls from, contracts you do not fully control (tokens, oracles, callbacks). Do not assume a token transfer returns true or that an oracle value is honest, and avoid acting on unchecked low-level call results. Actions: check the success flag and return data of every external call, use safe wrappers (SafeERC20) for non-standard tokens, validate oracle freshness/bounds, and isolate untrusted interactions. Validation signals include tests with a malicious mock token/oracle that returns false, reverts, or re-enters, all handled safely. Risk boundary: trusting an attacker-controlled contract's behavior is how price-manipulation and fake-token exploits work; treat unvalidated external return data on a value path as a high-severity gap.
